packageRules.description: Group all Actions bumps (the github-actions manager also covers .forgejo/workflows) into one PR.
Group the Python runtime + dev requirements (base / gpu / convert / dev) into one PR.

NVIDIA CUDA wheels: never cross the pinned major. main.py's Windows DLL preloader hardcodes cublas64_12 / cudnn64_9, so a major bump (cublas 13.x, cudnn 10.x) would silently break GPU inference. The <13 / <10 caps in requirements-gpu.txt already block this; this makes the intent explicit.

Keep workflow actions on floating major tags (@vN). Suppress pin/patch/minor so they are never pinned to an exact patch (upstream moves the @vN tag, so patch fixes land at runtime without a PR — and the local action mirrors only sync tags every 8h, so an exact-patch pin can race the mirror). A new MAJOR still surfaces via the rule below.

Major bumps require manual review — surface as checkboxes on the Dependency Dashboard, do not open the PR automatically, do not auto-merge. Minor/patch still flow through automerge gated by all-ci (which must be a required status check in the Forgejo branch protection for platformAutomerge to wait on it).
